Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Potential target for improving disease and lodging resistance through enhanced lignin content; candidate for marker-assisted selection for pathogen resistance
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression transgenic lines; dual luciferase reporter assay (promoter activation of OsPAL1, OsPAL2, OsPAL3); yeast one-hybrid assay (binding to OsPAL1, OsPAL2, OsPAL3, Os4CL3, Os4CL5 promoters); qRT-PCR of target genes; lignin content quantification (AcBr method); pathogen inoculation (Xoo)
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: R2R3-type MYB transcription factor; activates lignin biosynthetic pathway genes (OsPAL1, OsPAL2, OsPAL3, Os4CL3, Os4CL5)
Genes.Experimental_Materials: OsMYB110-OX transgenic lines (ubiquitin promoter, ZH11 background)
Genes.Expression_Pattern: High expression in plumules, radicles, roots, and mature leaves; co-expressed with lignin biosynthetic genes in cluster XII
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Increases flux toward lignin biosynthesis by transcriptionally activating PAL and 4CL genes
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Co-expression network analysis (RMRN); guilt-by-association using known regulator OsMYB30 as bait
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: OsMYB110 is highly homologous to the known lignin regulator OsMYB30 and shares co-regulated gene networks with OsMYB30 and OsMYB55.
Genes.Nutrient_Assay_or_Profiling_Method: Acetyl bromide (AcBr) method for lignin quantification
Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome (CREP, GSE19024); metabolome (OMIX510); co-expression network analysis
Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Enhanced disease resistance to bacterial blight (Xoo strains PXO99 and PXO341); OsMYB110-OX lines showed significantly smaller lesions
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: ~48% increase in lignin content in OsMYB110-OX leaves compared to ZH11
Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Transcriptional activation; directly binds promoters of OsPAL1, OsPAL2, OsPAL3, Os4CL3, Os4CL5
Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus (confirmed by YFP fusion and DAPI staining in rice protoplasts)
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMYB110 is an R2R3-MYB TF identified via co-expression network (RMRN) as co-expressed with lignin pathway genes in cluster XII. 2. OsMYB110 directly binds and activates the promoters of OsPAL1, OsPAL2, OsPAL3, Os4CL3, and Os4CL5 (confirmed by Y1H and dual-LUC assays). 3. Overexpression of OsMYB110 increases expression of lignin biosynthetic genes and results in ~48% higher lignin content in leaves. 4. OsMYB110-OX lines show enhanced resistance to Xoo bacterial blight.
Genes.Variety: ZH11 (transgenic background); Zhenshan 97 and Minghui 63 (expression profiling)

Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Potential target for improving disease and lodging resistance; bidirectional control of lignin (OX increases, RNAi decreases) confirmed
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and RNAi transgenic lines; qRT-PCR of target genes (Os4CL3, Os4CL5, OsPAL6, OsC4H); lignin content quantification (AcBr method); pathogen inoculation (Xoo)
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: R2R3-type MYB transcription factor; regulates lignin biosynthetic pathway genes (Os4CL3, Os4CL5, OsPAL6, OsC4H)
Genes.Experimental_Materials: OsMYB55-OX and OsMYB55-RNAi transgenic lines (ubiquitin promoter, ZH11 background)
Genes.Expression_Pattern: High expression in plumules, radicles, roots, and mature leaves; co-expressed with lignin biosynthetic genes
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Increases flux toward lignin biosynthesis; RNAi reduces flux
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Co-expression network analysis (RMRN); guilt-by-association using known regulator OsMYB30 as bait
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Note: The accession LOC_Os01g07120 is listed for OsMYB55 in the Methods section under 'Construction of transgenic lines', though this same accession is also mentioned for OsDREB2A in the text — this appears to be an error in the original paper. OsMYB55 is phylogenetically close to OsMYB30 and OsMYB110.
Genes.Nutrient_Assay_or_Profiling_Method: Acetyl bromide (AcBr) method for lignin quantification
Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome (CREP, GSE19024); metabolome (OMIX510); co-expression network analysis
Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Enhanced disease resistance to Xoo (PXO99 and PXO341) in OX lines; reduced resistance in RNAi lines
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: ~15% increase in OsMYB55-OX; ~13% decrease in OsMYB55-RNAi compared to ZH11
Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Transcriptional activation of lignin biosynthetic genes
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMYB55 is an R2R3-MYB TF co-expressed with lignin pathway genes, identified via RMRN. 2. OsMYB55-OX increases expression of Os4CL3, Os4CL5, OsPAL6, OsC4H and increases lignin content by ~15%. 3. OsMYB55-RNAi decreases expression of these genes and reduces lignin content by ~13%. 4. OsMYB55-OX enhances and RNAi reduces resistance to Xoo bacterial blight.

Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Novel regulator of seed glycerophospholipid composition; potential target for improving rice seed nutritional quality and abiotic stress tolerance; may be useful for breeding highly nutritional rice
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression transgenic lines; dual luciferase reporter assay (activation of OsPLAIVa, OsPLAIIIa, OsPLDα promoters); qRT-PCR of target genes; UPLC-MS/MS metabolite profiling of glycerophospholipids in seeds; abiotic stress assays (PEG6000, NaCl)
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: AP2/EREBP domain transcription factor (DREB subfamily); activates glycerophospholipid metabolism genes (OsPLAIVa, OsPLAIIIa, OsPLDα, OsMHZ11)
Genes.Experimental_Materials: OsDREB2A-OX transgenic lines (ubiquitin promoter, ZH11 background)
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Enriched in seeds (cluster VIII); co-expressed with glycerophospholipid metabolism genes and glycerophospholipid metabolites
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Increases flux toward glycerophospholipid degradation (diacyl → monoacyl) by activating phospholipase genes, resulting in increased LPC/LPE and decreased PC/PE/PS
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Unbiased co-expression network screening based on tissue specificity (RMRN cluster VIII, seed-specific)
Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Dehydration stress (PEG6000) and salt stress (NaCl) tested for OX lines
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: This is the first report linking a DREB subfamily TF to glycerophospholipid metabolism regulation in rice. The mechanism likely involves OsDREB2A activating phospholipase A family genes (OsPLAIVa, OsPLAIIIa, OsMHZ11) that catalyze hydrolysis of diacylglycerophospholipids to monoacylglycerophospholipids.
Genes.Nutrient_Trait_Metric: content; composition ratio (LPC/LPE increased, PC/PE/PS decreased)
Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome (CREP, GSE19024); metabolome (OMIX510); co-expression network analysis
Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Enhanced tolerance to dehydration (PEG6000) and salt stress (NaCl); higher survival rate and better shoot/root growth under stress in OsDREB2A-OX seedlings
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: Most monoacylglycerophospholipids (LPC and LPE) significantly higher in OsDREB2A-OX seeds; diacylglycerophospholipids (PC, PE, PS) lower; OsPLAIVa expression increased ~2-fold (P=3.5E-4)
Genes.Reaction_Product_or_Immediate_Output: Monoacylglycerophospholipids (LPC, LPE) via phospholipase-mediated hydrolysis of diacylglycerophospholipids (PC, PE, PS)
Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Transcriptional activation; recognizes DRE motif (A/GCCGAC core); activates promoters of glycerophospholipid metabolism genes
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsDREB2A is an AP2/EREBP TF identified via unbiased tissue-specific co-expression screening in RMRN cluster VIII (seed-specific). 2. OsDREB2A directly activates promoters of OsPLAIVa, OsPLAIIIa, and OsPLDα (dual-LUC assay). 3. OsDREB2A-OX increases expression of glycerophospholipid metabolism genes (OsPLAIVa ~2-fold, OsPLAIIIa, OsPLDα, OsMHZ11). 4. OsDREB2A-OX seeds show significantly increased monoacylglycerophospholipids (LPC, LPE) and decreased diacylglycerophospholipids (PC, PE, PS). 5. OsDREB2A-OX seedlings show enhanced tolerance to dehydration and salt stress.
Genes.Units_and_Basis: Relative metabolite content in seeds; gene expression fold-change
Genes.Variety: ZH11 (transgenic background); Zhenshan 97 and Minghui 63 (expression profiling)

Title: Rice metabolic regulatory network spanning the entire life cycle
